headline: FBTC is a Bitcoin-bridged ERC-20 token; contract deployed on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Mantle, and Base; limited on-chain address discovery without detailed deployment docs or block-explorer enumeration
short_headline: Bitcoin bridge token; multiple chains; addresses discovered: 1

findings.text: Block-explorer name-tag search for FBTC partially blocked; Etherscan search redirected; Ethereum contract address 0xc96de26018a54d51c097160568752c4e3bd6c364 confirmed via CoinGecko
Official docs at https://docs.fbtc.com/ confirm multi-chain deployment (Ethereum, Arbitrum, Mantle, Base, Optimism reported); no formal 'Deployments' page listing all addresses per chain found
Audit directory exists at https://github.com/fbtc-xyz/fbtc-contract/tree/main/audits but not fetchable; audit links not pre-recorded in protocol context
GitHub repo https://github.com/fbtc-xyz/fbtc-contract is public but script/ and audits/ directory contents not accessible; README minimal (build instructions only)

Protocol.chains lists 'Bitcoin' but FBTC operates as ERC-20 token on EVM chains (Ethereum, Arbitrum, Mantle, Base, Optimism) with Bitcoin reserves held separately; protocol is a bridge, not a Bitcoin L2
No factory pattern contracts identified in fetched sources; FBTC is a single token contract, not a factory-spawned system
Bridge contract role inferred from docs (minting, burning, merchant management); TSS nodes referenced for signing; no specific governance token or multisig address discovered
One address discovered: 0xc96de26018a54d51c097160568752c4e3bd6c364 (FBTC token on Ethereum); role: token; actor_class: unknown (ERC-20 proxy presumed but not verified on-chain)

rationale.verdict: Grade 'unknown' because: (1) protocol.chains mislabels 'Bitcoin' when FBTC is EVM-deployed; (2) no comprehensive deployed-contracts doc found listing all chain deployments with roles; (3) only 1 contract address verified (Ethereum FBTC token); (4) GitHub deployment artifacts (script/broadcast/) not accessible; (5) audit reports not fetched; (6) bridge control addresses (multisig, TSS gateway) not discovered on-chain. Width-limited discovery with strong evidence of deployment footprint but incomplete address enumeration.

evidence.shows: Official website confirms Function FBTC is a multi-chain Bitcoin yield asset; links to documentation, audits, and proof-of-assets pages; footer lists deployment chains as Ethereum, Mantle, and others

Documentation overview states FBTC is deployed on multiple blockchain networks including Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Mantle; token is ERC-20 standard; mentions bridge contract components but no address list
Bridge contract documentation describes merchant management, minting/burning requests, multi-signature security, and decentralized governance; no contract addresses listed
FBTC contract address on Ethereum listed as 0xc96de26018a54d51c097160568752c4e3bd6c364; trading pairs include WBTC/FBTC on Ethereum and Mantle; market cap data provided

Public repository with Solidity contracts (98.4%); includes audits/, contracts/, script/, and test/ directories; README is minimal (only Foundry setup instructions); no deployment addresses or audit details visible in fetched README

Bug bounty program confirms audits exist and can be found at https://github.com/fbtc-xyz/fbtc-contract/tree/main/audits; completed at 20 December 2024; mentions critical smart contract rewards up to $100k
Proof-of-assets page displays Bitcoin reserve balances and FBTC totals by chain and protocol; page structure present but data display requires dynamic loading (not visible in static fetch)
Ecosystem page lists merchant and vault manager programs; confirmed integration with multiple DeFi protocols; no specific contract addresses listed

unknowns: D1: Etherscan name-tag search blocked; direct per-chain block-explorer enumeration not performed for Arbitrum, Base, Mantle, Optimism
D2: No canonical 'Deployed Contracts' or 'Addresses' page found at /docs/deployments or similar paths; deployment addresses scattered across informal sources
D3: Audit PDF contents not fetched; audit directory exists but not accessible via fetch tool
D4: GitHub script/ directory (Foundry broadcast logs with deployment addresses) not accessible; no hardhat-deploy artifacts (deployments/<network>/Contract.json) found
D5: Protocol.chains field contains 'Bitcoin' but FBTC is ERC-20 deployed on EVM chains with separate Bitcoin reserve custody; architectural mismatch needs clarification
D5-offchain: No addresses discovered from Bitcoin blockchain explorers (FBTC is not deployed on Bitcoin mainnet)
D6: Factory contracts or pool enumeration not applicable; FBTC is single token, not factory pattern
D7: Bridge contract owner/admin address not discovered; TSS gateway and merchant management roles inferred from docs but addresses not retrieved
D8: Only 1 address discovered against claimed multi-chain deployment; Arbitrum, Mantle, Base, Optimism contract addresses not enumerated

protocol_metadata.about: Function FBTC is a multi-chain Bitcoin yield asset that brings native Bitcoin liquidity into DeFi. FBTC is fully 1:1 backed by Bitcoin reserves held in custody and uses ERC-20 tokens deployed on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Mantle, Base, and other EVM chains. The protocol employs Threshold Signature Schemes (TSS) and Multi-Party Computation (MPC) for bridge security, enabling Bitcoin holders to access yield-generating strategies without selling their BTC.
